page: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the system delivers 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and features a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The instance includes no local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but features a high-capacity 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6in.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring 124,964.56 in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and 87,698.18 in PassMark CPU tests. Its cryptographic capabilities are highly rated, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, which yield top-tier results in PassMark encryption benchmarks. Memory performance is strong, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 335.16 GB/sec. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1908.8 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1686.0.

The server is highly optimized for network-heavy, compute-intensive workloads. It achieves strong results in static web serving throughput (69.88 GB/sec) and database operations, alongside a top-tier Redis SET performance of 112.35 million operations per second. The lack of local NVMe storage means all disk I/O must traverse the network, though this is mitigated by the 200 Gbps network interface. The hardware profile is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput network appliances, while being less optimal for workloads requiring massive local storage or high single-threaded clock speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory and a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network interface, though it lacks local storage. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance, as demonstrated by its PassMark and stress-ng benchmarks, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of 335.16 GB/sec. Hardware-accelerated instructions like AVX-512 and AES-NI enhance its capability for vector calculations and secure data processing. This configuration is highly efficient for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, and high-volume Redis transactions.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-throughput, bare-metal compute platform featuring 128 vCPUs and 200 Gbps networking for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
256 GB of DDR4 memory with 335.16 GB/sec large-block read bandwidth
High-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for intensive I/O
Top-tier multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and Redis performance
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Best suited for high-throughput network appliances, databases, and multi-threaded compute
